text.title: nextjs-app-router Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nextjs-app-router as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding 'use client' to a page or layout that only needs interactivity in one child component.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Converts the entire route segment to client-rendered code, breaking SSR, streaming, and direct DB access for the whole page.

failure-mode.description: Accessing params.id synchronously in a dynamic page component trained on Next.js 13/14 patterns.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Next.js 15 changed params and searchParams to be Promises. Synchronous access returns undefined at runtime without a visible compile error.

failure-mode.description: Sending file uploads through a Server Action form.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Server Actions have a 1 MB default payload limit. Files larger than this will silently fail or error at runtime.

failure-mode.description: Importing Node.js-only modules (e.g., fs, path, crypto) in middleware.ts.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Middleware runs on the Edge runtime which has a limited Node.js API surface. Importing unavailable modules causes silent build failures.

failure-mode.description: Using matcher: ['/'] or matcher: ['/:path*'] in middleware config, intercepting static assets and API routes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Running middleware on every request including _next/static assets and public files adds latency and can break static asset delivery.
